questions.q: In Roman mythology, who is the king of the gods and god of thunder, equivalent to the Greek Zeus?
questions.explanation: Jupiter is the Roman king of the gods and controls thunder, directly equivalent to the Greek Zeus. Mars is the god of war, Neptune rules the sea, and Minerva is the goddess of wisdom.

questions.q: In Hindu mythology, who is the supreme reality and cosmic consciousness from which all existence emerges?
questions.explanation: Brahman is the ultimate reality and universal consciousness in Hindu philosophy and mythology. Brahma is the creator god (distinct from Brahman), Indra is the king of gods, and Shiva is a major deity.

questions.q: Which Celtic warrior is known in legend for defending Ulster against invasion, despite being under a curse?
questions.explanation: Cú Chulainn is the hero of Ulster Cycle who single-handedly defended Ulster. Finn mac Cumhaill is a hero of the Fenian Cycle, Oisín is a poet, and Balor is a giant.

questions.q: In Mesopotamian mythology, which god was king of the gods and responsible for creating order from chaos?
questions.explanation: Marduk is the Babylonian king of gods who defeated the chaos monster Tiamat and created order. Enlil was worshipped earlier, Enuma Elish is a creation myth text, and Tiamat is the chaos dragon.

questions.q: In Japanese mythology, what is the name of the sun goddess whose withdrawal caused eternal darkness?
questions.explanation: Amaterasu is the Japanese sun goddess whose retreat into a cave caused darkness; her emergence restored light. Susanoo is the storm god, Tsukuyomi is the moon god, and Raiden is a later thunder deity.

questions.q: In Celtic mythology, what is the name of the Otherworld or magical realm across the sea?
questions.explanation: Mag Mell and Tír na nÓg are both names for the Celtic Otherworld of eternal youth. Avalon is specifically King Arthur's isle, and Annwn is the Welsh underworld—related but distinct concepts.

questions.q: In Hindu mythology, what is the name of the epic war between two families, the Pandavas and Kauravas?
questions.explanation: The Mahabharata is the epic containing the Kurukshetra War between the Pandavas and Kauravas. The Ramayana is another epic, the Vedas are sacred texts, and the Bhagavad Gita is a section of the Mahabharata.

questions.q: In Roman mythology, who is the goddess of love and beauty, equivalent to the Greek Aphrodite?
questions.explanation: Venus is the Roman goddess of love and beauty, equivalent to Aphrodite. Diana is the goddess of the hunt, Juno is the queen of gods, and Vesta is the goddess of the hearth.

questions.q: In Japanese Shinto mythology, what refers to the divine spirits or gods that inhabit nature and objects?
questions.explanation: Kami are the divine spirits or deities in Shinto that inhabit natural phenomena and objects. Yōkai are supernatural creatures, Arashi means storm, and Daimyo refers to feudal lords.

questions.q: In Mesopotamian mythology, which goddess represents chaos and the primordial waters before creation?
questions.explanation: Tiamat is the chaos dragon and primordial water goddess in Babylonian creation mythology who was defeated by Marduk. Inanna is the goddess of love and war, Ninhursag is mother of gods, and Namtar is a plague god.
